title: Fix Website Workshop Copy and Current CTA
support: Marketing support or trusted copy reviewer
tool: Website editor + DSG Brand Kit + DSG Brief Builder
deliverable: Workshop page no longer promotes July 6-11 group workshops as available; current status, registrant-support path, and future-interest CTA are accurate.
context: The current operating picture shifted from promotion to cancellation/pivot cleanup. The public site is one of the most visible cleanup surfaces and has not been independently verified by the agent.
nextSteps: Open the website editor and identify any stale July 6-11 group workshop copy.
Replace unavailable registration language with current status and a future-interest or registrant-support path.
Avoid future dates until post-mortem, tracking, budget, and offer reset are approved.
Have Andrew approve exact public wording before publishing.
prompt: Create DSG website replacement copy for the July workshop page. The page should no longer promote July 6-11 group workshops as available. Write a warm, clear, accurate current-status note, a registrant-support path, and a future-interest CTA. Do not announce future dates. Keep the tone grounded, professional, and not overly dramatic. Return short web copy options and a review checklist.

title: Clean Up Zeffy Event and Registrant Records
tool: Zeffy + registration records + Google Sheet if needed
deliverable: Zeffy event and registration state checked, ticket sales paused/hidden/canceled as appropriate, attendee notes updated, and refund/transfer/1-on-1 handling documented without PII in the cockpit.
saveFinalIn: DSG Share Folder > Programming > Workshop Email Communication
context: Zeffy is the exact participant record source. The workbook repeatedly flags that the public event state and attendee notes have not been verified.
nextSteps: Open the Zeffy event and confirm whether registration is still public or active.
Pause, hide, or cancel stale registration as appropriate after Andrew confirms boundaries.
Use Zeffy for participant-specific records and avoid copying PII into the cockpit.
Document refund, transfer, or 1-on-1 handling status.
prompt: Create a DSG Zeffy cleanup checklist for canceled/pivoted July workshops. Include event visibility, ticket state, attendee notes, refund/transfer/1-on-1 handling, privacy rules, owner/support/approver, and what evidence should be saved. Do not copy participant PII into the cockpit.

title: Finish Direct Registrant Care Messages
tool: Email + Zeffy + Richard Meeting Prep + DSG Email Brief Builder
deliverable: Each registrant receives approved direct communication offering a clear path: refund, transfer, or 1-on-1 alternative; responses are logged safely.
saveFinalIn: DSG Share Folder > Programming > Workshop Email Communication
context: Board direction is direct registrant care rather than broad public cancellation. Richard can support participant review, but final option boundaries and sender/reply path need Andrew approval and Monae review.
nextSteps: Confirm the registrant list from Zeffy.
Confirm the refund, transfer, and 1-on-1 boundaries.
Draft warm direct messages from Richard or the appropriate sender.
Route final wording to Monae for review before sending.
Track each response and selected option outside the public Hub.
prompt: Create a DSG registrant-care email packet for current July workshop registrants. The message should be warm, honest, grounded, direct, and clear. Offer refund, transfer, or 1-on-1 alternative. Do not write a broad public cancellation announcement. Return plain text copy, branded HTML, subject line, preview text, review checklist, and response-tracking notes.

title: Audit Social, Buffer, Eventbrite, and Listings
tool: Buffer + Meta Business Suite + Facebook Page + Eventbrite/community listings
deliverable: Scheduled posts, public listings, Facebook/Page language, and community calendar copy are stopped, corrected, withdrawn, or clearly updated.
context: The workbook flags stale July 6-11 promo risk across Buffer, Facebook, Eventbrite, and community listings. Buffer recap still contained promo language, and listing state is not independently verified.
nextSteps: Audit Buffer scheduled and recent posts.
Check Meta/Facebook Page and Instagram references.
Check Eventbrite and any community calendars or listings.
Stop, update, withdraw, or comment where stale workshop promotion remains.
Do not make unsupported public claims about registrations, metrics, or future dates.
prompt: Create a DSG stale-public-copy audit checklist for Buffer, social channels, Facebook Page, Eventbrite, and community listings. Include what to check, what to stop or correct, what needs Andrew approval, and a public-claims rule. Keep it practical and suitable for delegated marketing support.

title: Confirm Ads Stopped and Archive Spend Evidence
support: Marketing support if platform screenshots are needed
approves: Clark for finance records; Andrew for ad/platform status
tool: Meta / Google ad dashboards + Relay + Google Drive
deliverable: Campaigns confirmed off with screenshot/export, final spend saved, four Facebook receipts handled, and learning notes captured for post-mortem.
context: Ads stopped is self-reported in the workbook, but final platform proof/export and spend evidence are missing. Four Relay Facebook receipt reminders are tracked.
nextSteps: Confirm Meta/Google campaigns are fully stopped.
Save screenshot or export of final campaign status and spend.
Upload or forward the four Facebook receipts to Relay.
Document any learning notes for the post-mortem.
Do not treat ad-stop status as verified until platform proof is saved.
prompt: Create a DSG ad-spend evidence checklist. Include ad-stop proof, screenshot/export requirements, final spend, Relay receipt handling, owner/support/approver, save location, and what should be summarized for the post-mortem.

title: Reconcile Zeffy Revenue, Payouts, and Refund Exposure
approves: Clark for finance records; Andrew for cancellation policy
deliverable: Zeffy June revenue/payouts matched to Relay deposits, and cancellation/refund/transfer exposure documented safely.
context: The workbook flags $100 raised/payouts in the June Zeffy report and at least one $20 purchase/payout that needs reconciliation against the cancellation plan.
nextSteps: Compare Zeffy June report with Relay deposits.
Document refund, transfer, or 1-on-1 exposure without participant PII.
Clarify any mismatch or blocker.
Save finance notes and receipts in the Finance folder.
prompt: Create a DSG Zeffy/Relay reconciliation checklist. Include June revenue, payouts, Relay deposits, refund/transfer exposure, privacy limits, owner/support/approver, due date, save location, and blocker note format.

title: Complete Raffle Gift Card and Winner Post Proof
deliverable: $50 Amazon card purchase/send proof saved, winner confirmation handled, and raffle winner post scheduled or published with approved team-safe language.
context: The workbook repeatedly warns not to mark the raffle/card tasks complete without purchase, send, and post evidence. Caption feedback and publish proof are still outstanding.
nextSteps: Confirm whether the $50 Amazon card was purchased.
Save receipt or order confirmation.
Confirm send path and recipient confirmation.
Finalize caption and publish or schedule the winner post.
Do not mark complete until proof exists.
prompt: Create a DSG raffle follow-through checklist. Keep gift-card purchase, gift-card send, and winner social post separate. Include proof needed before completion, owner/support/approver, caption review, save location, and a safe public-post language reminder.

title: Schedule Post-Mortem and Marketing Funnel Reset
approves: Andrew for meeting plan; Board for budget or strategy commitments
tool: Google Calendar + Google Docs/Sheets + Buffer/social/ad/Zeffy metrics
deliverable: Post-mortem scheduled with final metrics, funnel lessons, website/tracking needs, expert-cost options, and next-cycle decisions.
context: Future workshop dates should wait until after the cancellation post-mortem, offer reset, tracking plan, and any marketing budget/expert path are reviewed.
nextSteps: Schedule the post-mortem after the immediate public cleanup is stable.
Collect registration, social, listing, ad, and website data.
Identify what changed, what failed, and what should not be repeated.
Review whether outside marketing/platform support is needed.
Capture decisions before announcing future dates.
prompt: Create a DSG July workshop cancellation post-mortem agenda. Include metrics to collect, funnel review, social/listing review, ad-spend proof, audience engagement questions, website/tracking needs, expert-cost options, decisions needed before future dates, and a clean decision log section.

title: Compare Website Builder, Tracking, and Quiz Funnel Options
tool: Gamma review + website platform research + tracking plan
deliverable: Builder options compared for Google/Meta tracking, quiz/assessment funnel support, maintainability, cost, and migration effort.
context: Gamma is the active site builder/subscription, but the workbook flags future tracking and quiz/assessment funnel needs before the next campaign.
nextSteps: Document current Gamma limitations and strengths.
Compare alternative builders against tracking, quiz funnel, maintainability, cost, and migration effort.
Do not make a platform decision before the post-mortem.
Prepare a recommendation for Andrew review.
prompt: Create a DSG website builder comparison brief. Compare current Gamma setup and possible alternatives against Google/Meta tracking, quiz or assessment funnel support, maintainability, cost, migration effort, and team usability. Return a simple recommendation format, not a giant platform research dump.

title: Move Compliance Filing, Calendar Vote, and Minutes Forward
approves: Board where authority or vote is required
due: Next team call / e-vote / before filing
tool: NY Charities portal + ST-119.2 form + compliance calendar + board minutes
deliverable: NY Charities filing/payment status, ST-119.2 authority clarity, compliance calendar review/vote, and June 29 minutes approval path are all moving with clear owners.
saveFinalIn: DSG Share Folder > Finance and Meetings > Minutes
context: The workbook shows several related compliance/governance items: NY Charities payment authorized, ST-119.2 authority unclear, compliance calendar not adopted, and board minutes still draft.
nextSteps: Retry NY Charities portal and save receipt or blocker note.
Confirm ST-119.2 filing authority before preparing submission.
Send compliance calendar review packet and capture vote result.
Collect comments and prepare June 29 minutes for approval.
Keep draft/final distinction clean.
prompt: Create a DSG compliance and board follow-through brief. Include NY Charities payment/filing, ST-119.2 authority clarification, compliance calendar review/vote, June 29 minutes approval, owner/support/approver, due timing, save locations, and what needs Andrew or board review. Keep it procedural and factual.

title: Draft Role Clarity, Decision Trees, and Org Chart
approves: Andrew for operating proposal; Board if governance changes
deliverable: Draft role descriptions, decision trees, and master org chart ready for Andrew review.
context: The cockpit and accountability materials point away from heavier tooling first and toward clear roles, decision lanes, and operating rhythms.
nextSteps: Draft role descriptions.
Draft decision trees and decision-rights guardrails.
Prepare master org chart.
Send to Andrew for first review.
Escalate only governance-level changes to the board.
prompt: Create a DSG role clarity work plan. Include role descriptions, decision trees, master org chart, owner/support/approver, due date, save location, and review sequence. Keep it simple and designed to reduce Andrew being the bottleneck.

title: Capture Team Replies, Edits, and Blockers
deliverable: Team replies, edits, blockers, assignment questions, and decision needs logged back into the cockpit only when evidence supports the change.
context: Andrew sent the Team Next Steps email. The active work is now feedback/blocker capture, not recreating another broad pre-send brief.
nextSteps: Review replies to the Team Next Steps email.
Capture blockers, assignment edits, and questions.
Update assignments only with source-backed evidence.
Do not store raw private notes or unnecessary personal details.
Bring unresolved decisions to the next team call.
prompt: Create a DSG blocker-capture workflow for replies to the Team Next Steps email. Include what to log, what not to log, owner/support/approver, evidence rules, due timing, and a short update format for blockers, assignment changes, and decisions needed before the next team call.

title: Stage Funding Readiness Without Displacing Cleanup
support: Omari / Vaughn if guidance call moves forward
approves: Board for sensitive identity or sponsor-benefit language
due: 2026-07-03 first staged materials; CitizensNYC deadline July 27
tool: Google Docs + Google Sheets + Grants Folder
deliverable: IRS letter, budget, program description, impact metrics, equity narrative, concept note, CitizensNYC/NYCT fit notes, and approval flags staged in the funding folder.
context: Funding leads are active, but the workbook says they should not displace urgent cancellation, registrant, compliance, and finance cleanup. Sensitive identity/sponsor-benefit language needs approval before external use.
nextSteps: Stage IRS letter, budget, program description, and metrics.
Prepare equity narrative and concept note as internal drafts.
Prepare CitizensNYC and NYCT fit notes.
Flag identity and sponsor-benefit language for approval before external use.
Keep this behind the urgent public cleanup work.
prompt: Create a DSG funding readiness brief that stays below urgent cancellation cleanup. Include staged materials, CitizensNYC July 27 note, NYCT fit notes, owner/support/approver, sensitive-language review, save location, and a clear rule not to use identity or sponsor-benefit language externally without approval.

title: Keep Longer-Range Ideas Parked With Review Triggers
deliverable: Arts service orgs, live band open mics, musical sing-thru nights, busking workshop, artist resource page, work-study pathway, and future workshop relaunch stay parked with clear review triggers.
context: The workbook intentionally parks good future ideas so they do not compete with the urgent public cleanup and registrant-care firebreak.
nextSteps: Keep future ideas visible but not active.
Attach each item to a review trigger.
Move only one item active after website, Zeffy, social/listing, finance, registrant, and post-mortem work stabilizes.
Avoid using parking-lot ideas as escape from urgent cleanup.
prompt: Create a DSG parking-lot review list with triggers. Include arts service orgs, live band open mics, musical sing-thru nights, busking workshop, artist resource page, work-study pathway, and future workshop relaunch. Make clear what stays parked and what condition would move it active.
